what steps should you take prior to treating a patient ?
qas.a:
1. wash hands
2. Don ppe
3. time out/ patient name, SS#, and date of birth

qas.q: Basic exam setup includes what ?
qas.a: mouth mirrors, explorers, cotton tip pliers, perio-probe, gauze, and cotton rolls

qas.q: what position should the patients head be set in to take a bisecting angle exposure (bitewing)?
qas.a: the head should be positioned so that the occlusal surface of the maxillary teeth are in a horizontal plane

qas.q: what is the four handed dentistry technique?
qas.a: the chairside technique that involves four hands (doc & assistant) working together to provide treatment to the oral cavity. transfer instruments, decrease chair time for the PT, relieve stress for everyone, and increase productivity.

qas.a: central incisor, lateral incisor, cuspids, bicuspids, molars

qas.q: define gingivitis
what is dental classification: Class I?
qas.a: Had an exam and needs no further treatment.

qas.q: what is dental classification: Class II?
qas.a: had an exam, needs treatment, but none that will require emergency care within the next 12 months.

qas.q: what is dental classification: Class III?
qas.a: had exam, needs treatment that will require emergency care within the next 12 months.

qas.a: flumazenil reverses the effects of benzodiazepines.
